brief_justification: Agent chose (B) instead of correct answer (A) — the problem states 80% effectiveness as a population-level parameter, so Re=0.6 is valid as given, but agent wrongly introduced coverage variable c not present in the problem; Agent goes beyond surface by introducing Re = R0×(1−efficacy×coverage) formula, showing understanding of coverage vs efficacy distinction even if misapplied; Agent proceeds linearly without questioning whether the problem intends population-level or individual-level efficacy; Agent provides the corrected formula but never checks whether its own reinterpretation of the problem is warranted; Agent acknowledges the distinction between individual and population parameters, showing some epistemic awareness; Agent addresses the formula directly and explains why it believes it requires coverage, giving a specific reason for rejection; Agent shows the derivation and coverage formula clearly, making the reasoning chain reproducible; Agent avoids the correlation-causation leap from the negative gate and doesn't conflate observational with interventional data; Agent avoids both suppressed failure modes (observational_causation_inference, correlation_to_causation_leap) throughout its analysis; Reasoning is mostly linear rather than following the DAG branches (observational/interventional gate, backdoor path analysis) specified in the topology.
